Identification.LongTitle: An Act to protect, maintain, revitalize and strengthen Indigenous languages

Provision.Text: This enactment provides, among other things, that

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the Government of Canada recognizes that the rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 and by the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples include rights related to Indigenous languages;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the Minister of Canadian Heritage may enter into different types of agreements or arrangements in respect of IndigenÂ­ous languages with Indigenous governments or other InÂ­digenous governing bodies or Indigenous organizations, taking into account the unique circumstances and needs of Indigenous groups, communities and peoples; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: federal institutions may cause documents to be translated into an Indigenous language or provide interpretation services to facilitate the use of an Indigenous language.
The enactment also establishes the Office of the Commissioner of Indigenous Languages and sets out its composition. The Officeâs mandate and powers, duties and functions include

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: supporting the efforts of Indigenous peoples to reclaim, revitalize, maintain and strengthen Indigenous languages;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: promoting public awareness of, among other things, the richness and diversity of Indigenous languages;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: undertaking research or studies in respect of the provision of funding for the purposes of supporting Indigenous languages and in respect of the use of Indigenous languages in Canada;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: providing services, including mediation or other culturally appropriate services, to facilitate the resolution of disputes; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: submitting to the Minister of Canadian Heritage an annual report on, among other things, the use and vitality of IndigenÂ­ous languages in Canada and the adequacy of funding provided by the Government of Canada for initiatives related to Indigenous languages.

TableOfProvisions.Label: â

Heading.TitleText: Powers, Duties and Functions of Minister

TableOfProvisions.Text: Consultations â adequate, sustainable and long-term funding
Agreements or arrangements â purposes of Act

Heading.TitleText: Office of Commissioner of Indigenous Languages

TableOfProvisions.Text: Commissioner absent or unable to act

Heading.TitleText: Mandate and Powers, Duties and Functions

TableOfProvisions.Text: Rules â Officeâs meetings and activities
Rules â dispute resolution and complaints

Provision.Text: Whereas the recognition and implementation of rights related to Indigenous languages are at the core of reconciliation with Indigenous peoples and are fundamental to shaping the country, particularly in light of the Truth and Reconciliation Commission of Canadaâs Calls to Action;
Whereas the Parliament of Canada is committed to implementing the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples, which affirms rights related to Indigenous languages;

Whereas 2019 has been proclaimed by the General Assembly of the United Nations as the International Year of Indigenous Languages to, among other things, draw attention to the critical loss of IndigenÂ­ous languages and the urgent need to maintain, revitalize and promote Indigenous languages;

Whereas the Parliament of Canada recognizes that all relations with Indigenous peoples must be based on the recognition and implementation of their right to self-determination, including the inherent right of self-government;

Whereas First Nations, the Inuit and the MÃ©tis Nation have their own collective identities, cultures and ways of life and have, throughout history and to this day, continued to live in, use and occupy the lands that are now in Canada;
Whereas Indigenous languages were the first languages spoken in the lands that are now in Canada and those languages have evolved over time;
Whereas Indigenous languages played a significant part in the establishment of relations between Europeans and Indigenous peoples;

Whereas Indigenous peoples have played a significant role in the development of Canada and Indigenous languages contribute to the diversity and richness of the linguistic and cultural heritage of Canada;

Whereas the Sixties Scoop and other discriminatory government policies and practices, in respect of, among other things, assimilation, forced relocation and residential schools, were detrimental to IndigenÂ­ous languages and contributed significantly to the erosion of those languages;

Whereas the status of Indigenous languages varies from one language to another, including with respect to the vitality of those languages, and there is an urgent need to support the efforts of Indigenous peoples to reclaim, revitalize, maintain and strengthen them;
Whereas Indigenous languages are fundamental to the identities, cultures, spirituality, relationships to the land, world views and self-determination of Indigenous peoples;

Whereas efforts to protect the vitality of Indigenous languages contribute to the enrichment of IndigenÂ­ous knowledge as well as to the prevention of the loss of cultural diversity, biodiversity and spirituality;

Whereas a variety of entities in different regions across Canada have mandates to promote the use of Indigenous languages and to support the efforts of Indigenous peoples to reclaim, revitalize, maintain and strengthen them and there is a need to provide continuing support for those entities;
Whereas the Government of Canada is committed to providing adequate, sustainable and long-term funding for the reclamation, revitalization, maintenance and strengthening of Indigenous languages;
Whereas Indigenous peoples are best placed to take the leading role in reclaiming, revitalizing, maintaining and strengthening Indigenous languages;

Whereas Indigenous-language media and lifelong learning of Indigenous languages, including education systems for Indigenous people, are essential to restoring and maintaining fluency in those languages;

Whereas a flexible approach that takes into account the unique circumstances and needs of Indigenous groups, communities and peoples is required in light of the diversity of identities, cultures and histories of Indigenous peoples;

And whereas there is a need to take into account the unique circumstances and needs of Indigenous elders, youth, children, persons with disabilities, women, men and gender-diverse persons and two-spirit persons;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Indigenous Languages Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: appropriate Ministerâhas the same meaning as in section 2 of the Financial Administration Act.â(ministre compÃ©tent)
Commissionerâmeans the Commissioner of Indigenous Languages appointed under section 13. (English version only)

federal institutionâmeans an entity that is referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (b) and (d) of the definition department in section 2 of the Financial Administration Act or a Crown corporation, as defined in subsection 83(1) of that Act.â(institution fÃ©dÃ©rale)

Indigenous governing bodyâmeans a council, government or other entity that is authorized to act on behalf of an Indigenous group, community or people that holds rights recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.â(corps dirigeant autochtone)

Indigenous organizationâmeans an Indigenous entity that represents the interests of an Indigenous group and its members or, other than in section 45, that is specialized in Indigenous languages.â(organisme autochtone)
Indigenous peoplesâhas the meaning assigned by the definition aboriginal peoples of Canada in subsection 35(2) of the Constitution Act, 1982.â(peuples autochtones)
Ministerâmeans the Minister of Canadian Heritage.â(ministre)
Officeâmeans the Office of the Commissioner of IndigenÂ­ous Languages established under section 12. (English version only)

Section.Text: This Act is to be construed as upholding the rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, and not as abrogating or derogating from them.

In the event of any inconsistency or conflict between this Act and a treaty â including a land claims agreement â or a self-government agreement, the treaty or self-government agreement prevails to the extent of the inconsistency or conflict.
The purposes of this Act are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: support and promote the use of Indigenous languages;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: support the efforts of Indigenous peoples to reclaim, revitalize, maintain and strengthen Indigenous languages, including their efforts to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: assess the status of Indigenous languages,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: plan initiatives and activities for restoring and maintaining fluency in Indigenous languages,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: create technological tools, educational materials and permanent records of Indigenous languages, including audio and video recordings of fluent speakers of the languages and written materials such as dictionaries, lexicons and grammars of the languages, for the purposes of, among other things, the maintenance and transmission of the languages,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: support Indigenous language learning and cultural activities, including language nest and immersion programs,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: support entities specialized in Indigenous languages, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: undertake research or studies in respect of Indigenous languages;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: establish a framework to facilitate the effective exerÂ­cise of the rights of Indigenous peoples that relate to Indigenous languages, including by way of agreements or arrangements referred to in sections 8 and 9;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: establish measures to facilitate the provision of adequate, sustainable and long-term funding for the reclamation, revitalization, maintenance and strengthening of Indigenous languages;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: facilitate cooperation with provincial governments, Indigenous governments and other Indigenous governing bodies, Indigenous organizations and other entities in a manner consistent with the powers and jurisdictions of the provinces and of Indigenous governing bodies and the rights of Indigenous peoples;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respond to the Truth and Reconciliation Commission of Canadaâs Calls to Action numbers 13 to 15; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: advance the achievement of the objectives of the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of IndigenÂ­ous Peoples as it relates to Indigenous languages.

Section.Text: The Government of Canada recognizes that the rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982 and by the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples include rights related to Indigenous languages.

Heading.TitleText: Powers, Duties and Functions of Minister

Section.MarginalNote: Consultations â adequate, sustainable and long-term funding
Section.Text: The Minister must consult with diverse Indigenous governments and other Indigenous governing bodies and diverse Indigenous organizations in order to meet the objective of providing adequate, sustainable and long-term funding for the reclamation, revitalization, maintenance and strengthening of Indigenous languages.

The Minister may cooperate with provincial governments, Indigenous governments or other Indigenous governing bodies, Indigenous organizations or other entities â including by entering into agreements or arrangements with them â to coordinate efforts to efficiently and effectively support Indigenous languages in Canada in a manner consistent with the powers and jurisdictions of the provinces and of Indigenous governing bodies and the rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982.

Section.MarginalNote: Agreements or arrangements â purposes of Act
Section.Text: Taking into account the unique circumstances and needs of Indigenous groups, communities and peoples and the research or studies referred to in section 24 and in a manner consistent with the powers and jurisdictions of the provinces and of Indigenous governing bodies and the rights of Indigenous peoples recognized and affirmed by section 35 of the Constitution Act, 1982, the Minister and an appropriate Minister may enter into an agreement or arrangement to further the purposes of this Act with a provincial government, an Indigenous government or other Indigenous governing body or an Indigenous organization.
For greater certainty, sections 8 and 9, and any agreement referred to in those sections, are not to be construed as preventing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any person or entity from relying on a provision relating to the reclamation, revitalization, maintenance or strengthening of an Indigenous language that is contained in a treaty â including a land claims agreement â or a self-government agreement; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an Indigenous government or other Indigenous governing body from entering into, with the Government of Canada or the government of a province, a treaty â including a land claims agreement â or a self-government agreement that provides, among other things, for the reclamation, revitalization, maintenance or strengthening of an Indigenous language.

Section.Text: A federal institution may cause

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any document under its control to be translated into an Indigenous language; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: interpretation services to be provided to facilitate the use of an Indigenous language in the course of the federal institutionâs activities.

Heading.TitleText: Office of Commissioner of Indigenous Languages

Subsection.Text: There is established an office, to be known as the Office of the Commissioner of Indigenous Languages, consisting of the Commissioner and up to three directors.

The Office is neither an agent of Her Majesty in right of Canada nor is it an entity governed by the Financial Administration Act, and its Commissioner, directors and employees are not part of the federal public administration.

On the recommendation of the Minister, the Governor in Council is to appoint a Commissioner of Indigenous Languages to hold office during good behaviour for a term not exceeding five years, subject to removal by the Governor in Council at any time for cause.
Before making a recommendation under subsection (1), the Minister must consult with diverse Indigenous governments and other Indigenous governing bodies and diverse Indigenous organizations.
The Commissioner must be an Indigenous person.

Section.Text: The Commissioner is the chief executive officer of the Office and has the control and management of the Office and all matters connected with it.

Section.MarginalNote: Commissioner absent or unable to act
Section.Text: If the Commissioner is absent or unable to act or if the office of Commissioner is vacant, the Minister may designate a director to act as Commissioner, but that director may act as Commissioner for a period of more than 90 days only with the approval of the Governor in Council given on the recommendation of the Minister made after the Minister has consulted with diverse Indigenous governments and other Indigenous governing bodies and diverse Indigenous organizations.

On the recommendation of the Minister made after the Minister has consulted with diverse Indigenous governments and other Indigenous governing bodies and diverse Indigenous organizations, the Governor in Council is to appoint up to three directors to hold office during pleasure for a term not exceeding five years.
The Commissioner and directors may be reappointed for a second or subsequent term of office.
The Commissioner is to be appointed to hold office on a full-time basis and the directors are to be appointed to hold office on either a full-time or a part-time basis.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner and directors are to be paid the remuneration determined by the Governor in Council.

The Commissioner is to be reimbursed for reasonable travel and other expenses incurred in performing duties while absent from the Commissionerâs ordinary place of work. The directors are to be reimbursed for such expenses incurred in performing duties while absent from their ordinary place of residence.

Section.Text: The Office may hire any employees that are necessary to carry out the activities of the Office and determine the duties of those persons and the conditions of their employment, including their remuneration and benefits.

The Commissioner, directors and employees of the Office must not accept or hold an office or employment that is inconsistent with their duties or take part in any matter that involves the Office and in which they have an interest.

The Officeâs head office is to be in the National Capital Region, as described in the schedule to the National Capital Act, or at any other place in Canada that may be designated by order of the Governor in Council.

Heading.TitleText: Mandate and Powers, Duties and Functions

Section.Text: The mandate of the Office is to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: help promote Indigenous languages;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: support the efforts of Indigenous peoples to reclaim, revitalize, maintain and strengthen Indigenous languages;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: facilitate the resolution of disputes and review complaints to the extent provided by this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: promote public awareness and understanding in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the diversity and richness of Indigenous languages,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the inseparable link between Indigenous languages and the cultures of Indigenous peoples,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the rights of Indigenous peoples related to Indigenous languages,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the significance of those rights for Indigenous peoples, as well as for the general public,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the negative impact of colonization and discriminatory government policies on Indigenous languages and the exercise of those rights, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the importance of working toward and contributing to reconciliation with Indigenous peoples; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: support innovative projects and the use of new technologies in Indigenous language education and revitalization, in cooperation with Indigenous governments and other Indigenous governing bodies, InÂ­digenous organizations, the Government of Canada and provincial governments.

Subsection.Text: The Office may undertake research or studies, or cause research or studies to be undertaken, in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the provision of funding for the purposes of supporting Indigenous languages; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the use of Indigenous languages in Canada, including for the purposes of measuring the vitality of those languages or identifying measures to restore and maintain fluency in those languages.

Subsection.Text: The research or studies referred to in paragraph (1)(b) may take into account, among other things, community assessments.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Statistics Canada and Library and Archives Canada
Subsection.Text: The Office may cooperate with Statistics Canada or Library and Archives Canada in relation to the research or studies referred to in subsection (1).

Section.Text: At the request of an Indigenous community or an Indigenous government or other Indigenous governing body, the Office may provide support to the community or the Indigenous government or other Indigenous governing body in its efforts to reclaim, revitalize, maintain and strengthen an Indigenous language, including its efforts to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: create permanent records of the language, including audio and video recordings and written materials such as dictionaries, lexicons and grammars of the language, for the purposes of, among other things, its maintenance and transmission;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establish certification standards for translators and interpreters;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: conduct community assessments in respect of the use of the language;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: develop and implement plans for reclaiming, revitalizing, maintaining and strengthening the language; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: engage with the Government of Canada or provincial governments to establish culturally appropriate methods of teaching and learning the language.

Section.Text: At the request of an Indigenous community, an Indigenous government or other Indigenous governing body, an Indigenous organization or the Government of Canada, the Office may provide services, including mediation or other culturally appropriate services, to facilitate the resolution of a dispute related to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the fulfilment by any party of an obligation related to Indigenous languages under an agreement entered into by the Government of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: funding provided by the Government of Canada for the purposes of an initiative related to Indigenous languages;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fulfilment of any of the Government of Canadaâs obligations under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the implementation of any of the Government of Canadaâs policies and programs related to Indigenous languages.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner may conduct a review of a complaint, filed by an Indigenous government or other Indigenous governing body, an Indigenous organization or an Indigenous person, respecting any matter referred to in any of paragraphs 26(a) to (d).
After conducting the review, the Commissioner must prepare a report containing any recommendations that the Commissioner considers to be appropriate.

Section.Text: The Office has the capacity, rights, powers and privÂ­ileges of a natural person, including the capacity to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enter into contracts;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: acquire, hold and dispose of property or an interest or right in property, or lease property; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: sue and be sued.

Section.Text: The Commissioner may delegate any of the Commissionerâs powers, duties and functions under this Act to any director or employee of the Office, subject to any conditions that the Commissioner may impose.

No action or other proceeding lies against the Commissioner, a director, an employee of the Office or a person acting on behalf of the Office for anything done, or omitted to be done, in the exercise or purported exercise in good faith of any power, or in the performance or purported performance in good faith of any duty or function, of that person in accordance with this Act.
The Officeâs fiscal year is the period beginning on April 1 in one year and ending on March 31 in the next year.

Subject to any conditions that the Treasury Board may impose, the Office may expend, in the furtherance of its mandate, during a fiscal year or the following one, any revenues that it receives in that fiscal year through the conduct of its activities.

Subsection.Text: The Office must establish a business plan and budget for each fiscal year and submit them to the Minister.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Scope and contents of business plan
Subsection.Text: The Officeâs business plan must encompass all of the Officeâs activities and include a statement of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Officeâs mandate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Officeâs objectives for the fiscal year and the measures it intends to employ to achieve those objectives; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Officeâs expected performance for the fiscal year.

Subsection.Text: The Officeâs budget must include a statement of the Officeâs projected revenues and expenses for the fiscal year.
The Office must carry on its activities in a fiscal year in a manner that is consistent with its business plan for that year.
The Office must keep books of account and records in relation to them and must also maintain financial and management control and information systems.
The books, records and systems referred to in subsection (1) must be kept and maintained in a manner that will provide reasonable assurance that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Officeâs assets are safeguarded and controlled;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: its transactions are carried out in accordance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: its financial, human and physical resources are managed economically and efficiently; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: its activities are carried out effectively.

Subsection.Text: The Office may cause internal audits to be conducted to assess the Officeâs transactions and activities for the purpose of verifying compliance with subsections (1) and (2).
The Office must prepare financial statements annually, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.
The Officeâs financial statements must be prepared in a form that clearly sets out information according to its principal activities.
The Office must cause an annual auditorâs report to be prepared on its financial statements and any quantitative information required to be audited under subsection (3).
The report must, among other things,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: include separate statements as to whether in the opinion of the Officeâs auditor

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the financial statements are presented fairly, in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, applied on a basis consistent with that of the preceding year,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the quantitative information is accurate in all material respects and, if applicable, was prepared on a basis consistent with that of the preceding year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Officeâs transactions that have come to the auditorâs notice in the course of the auditorâs examination for the report were carried out in accordance with this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: call attention to any other matter falling within the scope of the auditorâs examination for the report that, in the auditorâs opinion, should be brought to the attention of the Office or the Minister.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may require that any quantitative information required to be included in the report under paragraph (2)(a) be audited.
The Office must submit to the Minister its audited financial statements with respect to the fiscal year within 90 days after the end of that fiscal year.

At any time required by the Minister, the Office must cause a special examination to be carried out in respect of its transactions and activities to determine if the books, records, systems and practices referred to in section 34 were, in the period under examination, maintained in a manner that met the requirements of that section.

The special examination is to be carried out by the Officeâs auditor. However, if, in the Ministerâs opinion, a person other than the Officeâs auditor should carry out the special examination, the Minister may, after consultation with the Commissioner, direct that the examination be carried out by another auditor who is qualified for the purpose.

Before commencing the special examination, an examÂ­iner must survey the Officeâs systems and practices to be examined and submit a plan for the examination, including a statement of the criteria to be applied in the examination, to the Commissioner.
The examiner must, to the extent feasible, rely on any internal audit conducted under subsection 34(3).
The examiner must, on completion of the special examination, submit a report on the examinerâs findings, and a summary of that report, to the Minister and the Commissioner.
The examinerâs report is to include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement as to whether, in the examinerâs opinion, having regard to the criteria referred to in subsection 34(2), there is a reasonable assurance that there are no significant deficiencies in the systems and practices examined; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement on the extent to which the examiner relied on internal audits.

Subsection.Text: The Office must, as soon as feasible after the Commissioner has received the report, post the summary of the report on its website.

Section.Text: The Officeâs auditor or the examiner may at any time consult the Auditor General of Canada on any matter relating to an audit or special examination.

Subsection.Text: At the request of the Officeâs auditor or the examiner, the present or former Commissioner, directors or employees of the Office must provide any information and explanations, and give access to any of the Officeâs records, documents, books, accounts and vouchers that are under their control, that the auditor or examiner considers necessary to prepare a report required under this Act.

If the Officeâs present or former Commissioner, directors or employees do not have information or explanations requested by an auditor or examiner under subsection (1), the Officeâs current Commissioner or directors must obtain the information or explanation and provide it to the auditor or examiner.

Section.Text: Nothing in sections 34 to 39 is to be construed as authorizing the Officeâs auditor or the examiner to express any opinion on the merits of matters of policy, including the merits of the Officeâs mandate or any policy decision of the Office.
An oral or written statement or a report made under this Act by the Officeâs auditor or an examiner has qualified privilege.

The Commissioner must, as soon as feasible, notify the Minister of any financial or other developments that, in the Commissionerâs opinion, are likely to have a maÂ­terial effect on the Officeâs ability to carry out its mandate or exercise its powers or perform its duties or functions, on its performance or on its requirements for funding.

Subsection.Text: The Office must, within four months after the end of each fiscal year, submit to the Minister an annual report on

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the use and vitality of Indigenous languages in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the needs of Indigenous groups, communities and peoples and entities that are specialized in Indigenous languages â and the progress made â in relation to the reclamation, revitalization, maintenance and strengthening of Indigenous languages;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the adequacy of funding provided by the Government of Canada for the purposes of initiatives related to Indigenous languages; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the implementation of this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Activities of Office and other contents
Subsection.Text: The annual report must also set out information respecting the Officeâs principal activities for the fiscal year and must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Officeâs financial statements;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the annual auditorâs report;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a statement on the extent to which the Office has met its objectives for the fiscal year; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other information specified by the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must cause a copy of the Officeâs report to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the day on which the report is received.

After it is tabled, the report stands referred to the committee of the Senate, the House of Commons or both Houses of Parliament that may be designated or established for the purpose of reviewing the report.

Section.Text: Following consultations by the Minister with the Office, diverse Indigenous governments and other IndigenÂ­ous governing bodies and diverse Indigenous organizations in order to take into account the unique circumstances and needs of Indigenous groups, communities and peoples, the Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting complaints referred to in section 27, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the filing of complaints, including the conditions that must be met before complaints may be filed,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the reports made after conducting the review of complaints, including the type of recommendations that may be made in those reports, the period of time within which the reports must be completed and the persons or entities to which the reports must be provided;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: specifying other information to be included in the annual report for the purposes of paragraph 43(2)(d); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: generally, for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Rules â Officeâs meetings and activities
Section.Text: The Office may make rules with respect to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the calling of meetings of the Office, the number of persons that constitute a quorum at each meeting and the manner in which decisions are to be made; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the conduct of the activities of the Office.

Section.MarginalNote: Rules â dispute resolution and complaints
Section.Text: Subject to any regulations made under paragraph 45(a), the Office may make rules, including rules of procedure, that apply in respect of dispute resolution services or the review of complaints.
The Office must make rules to ensure the confidentiality of the information the Office receives in confidence in the exercise of its powers and the performance of its duties and functions.

Subsection.Text: Within five years after the day on which this section comes into force and every five years after that, the Minister must cause to be conducted an independent review of this Act, of its administration and operation, of any agreements or arrangements made under section 9 and of the activities of the Office. The review must be conducted by a person or body appointed by the Minister in consultation with the Office.
The person or body that conducts the review must submit to the Minister a report setting out their conclusions and recommendations, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any measures that would contribute to the fulfilment of the objectives of this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any changes to this Act in relation to, among other things, the mandate or the activities of the Office or the agreements or arrangements made under section 9.

Subsection.Text: The Minister must cause a copy of the report to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the day on which the report is received.

After it is tabled, the report stands referred to the committee of the Senate, the House of Commons or both Houses of Parliament that may be designated or established for the purpose of reviewing the report.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force 90 days after the day on which it receives royal assent.
